description:
# Same play as .43.D, applied to lower_case

## What changes

`lower_case` (src/ir_lower.rs ~2252):
- builds `Matrix { subjects: vec![subj_v], rows: clauses }` (1-column)
- calls `lower_pattern_matrix` with body lowering callback
- removes per-clause `try_blocks` cascade

Tests of guard handling, cont_fn body lift, etc. inherit from .D's
infrastructure.

## What stays

- The case body's cont_fn lift behavior (from fz-duq.3).
- The `fail_block` semantics.

## What's removed

- Per-clause cascade in lower_case.
- The duplication of cascade logic between lower_multi_clause (already
  gone post-.D) and lower_case.

## Acceptance

- All case-using fixtures: golden byte-identical OR strictly smaller.
- Three-path parity.
- Existing lower_case unit tests pass unchanged.

## Risk

lower_case has been the trickiest call site historically (cont_fn lift
work). The matrix integration is mechanical at this point — same
callback pattern as .D.
title: fz-ul4.43.F: wire lower_case onto matrix
